label: Day 2 — Afternoon: Environmental Awareness Workshop
description: The first content session of the exchange. Before diving into green habits and sustainable lifestyles, we need to understand where participants actually stand: what do they know, what do they think they know, and what are the gaps? This session is diagnostic and provocative — designed to spark curiosity, not to lecture.
objectives: Assess the group's baseline knowledge of environmental issues and sustainability
Challenge assumptions and common misconceptions about green living
Create a shared vocabulary for the rest of the exchange
Inspire curiosity and personal connection to the topic

steps.title: Four Corners — Where do you stand?
steps.body: Statements are read aloud. Participants move to corners: Strongly Agree / Agree / Disagree / Strongly Disagree. Brief discussion at each corner. No right answers yet — just positions.

steps.title: Sustainability Quiz
steps.body: Teams compete on the interactive quiz below — 15 questions across categories: climate facts, local green heroes, eco-myths, Istanbul & Turkey, global stats. Fast-paced, scored in real time.

steps.title: Myth Busters
steps.body: Each team receives 3 'green myths'. They have 10 minutes to discuss and build their argument. Then each team presents — facilitator reveals the nuanced truth after each one.

steps.title: Personal connection
steps.body: Silent reflection: each participant writes one environmental issue they feel personally connected to and one they know nothing about. Posted on the wall — visible all week.

facilitatorNote: Avoid presenting yourself as the expert. Your role here is to generate productive confusion — to make participants aware of what they don't know without making them feel stupid. Celebrate wrong answers as much as right ones.
